questions.stem: I ______ my homework two hours ago, but I ______ it yet.
questions.explanation: 第一空后有'two hours ago'（两小时前），是具体的过去时间状语，必须用一般过去时finished；第二空后有'y et'（尚未），是现在完成时的标志词，且为否定形式haven't finished。解题技巧：看到'ago'选过去时，看到'y et/already/ever/never'选现在完成时。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The boy ______ is playing basketball over there is my cousin.
questions.explanation: 先行词是'the boy'指人，排除B（which指物）；定语从句'is playing basketball'缺少主语，且关系词在从句中作主语，用who。whom只能在从句中作宾语，whose作定语表所属关系。口诀：先行词指人用who/that，指物用which/that，从句缺主语用who/which，缺宾语可用whom。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: These library books ______ by students tomorrow morning.
questions.explanation: 时间状语'tomorrow morning'表明用一般将来时；主语'books'与动词'return'是被动关系（书被归还），所以用一般将来时的被动语态will be returned。被动语态结构：be+过去分词，时态通过be的变化体现。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The book ______ cover is red belongs to my sister.
questions.explanation: 本题考查定语从句中的所属关系。空格后是'cover'（封面），需要定语修饰，表示'the book's cover'（这本书的封面）。whose在定语从句中作定语，表示'……的'，既可指人也可指物。which和that在从句中作主语或宾语，不能作定语；who指人。解题关键：看到空格后紧跟名词，考虑whose表示所属。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: If you don't know the word, you can ______ in the dictionary.
questions.explanation: look up表示'查阅（词典、资料）'，find out表示'查明、发现（事实、真相）'。查单词用look up。代词it要放在look和up中间，名词可放中间或后面（look the word up / look up the word）。口诀：动词+副词短语，代词放中间，名词放两边。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The movie is ______. I feel very ______ when watching it.
questions.explanation: -ing形容词（exciting/boring/interesting）修饰事物，表示'令人……的'；-ed形容词（excited/bored/interested）修饰人，表示'感到……的'。第一空主语是movie（物），用exciting；第二空主语是I（人），用excited。口诀：-ing形容物，-ed形容人。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The ______ of the mountain is covered with snow all year round.
questions.explanation: 根据句意'终年被雪覆盖'，结合常识，山顶（top）才会有常年积雪。foot指山脚，middle指山腰，bottom指底部。解题技巧：利用上下文线索和常识推理，'covered with snow all year round'是高海拔地区的特征。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 此处需要表示对比关系，'一些学生用工具捡垃圾，而另一些分类装袋'。while表示'而、却'，用于对比两件事同时发生或两种情况对比。and表并列，but表转折，or表选择。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last term, our school set up an Environmental Protection Club. As a member, I have learned a lot about how to protect our environment.

One Saturday, we organized a clean-up activity in the city park. When we arrived, we were shocked to see so much rubbish everywhere. Plastic bottles and paper bags were scattered under the trees.

We quickly got to work. Some students used tools to pick up the rubbish, (8)_______ others sorted it into different bags. At first, I felt tired and wanted to give up. (9)_______, I remembered why we were there. The park is home to many birds, and they need a clean place to live.

After three hours, the park looked completely different. Some visitors even (10)_______ us and said we were doing a great job. This experience taught me that small actions can make a big (11)_______. I have realized that everyone (12)_______ play a part in protecting our planet.

questions.explanation: 前句说'我想放弃'，后句说'我想起为什么来这里'，前后是转折关系。However表示'然而'，符合语境。Therefore因此，Besides此外，Instead代替。注意：however通常用逗号与句子其他部分隔开。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last term, our school set up an Environmental Protection Club. As a member, I have learned a lot about how to protect our environment.

One Saturday, we organized a clean-up activity in the city park. When we arrived, we were shocked to see so much rubbish everywhere. Plastic bottles and paper bags were scattered under the trees.

We quickly got to work. Some students used tools to pick up the rubbish, (8)_______ others sorted it into different bags. At first, I felt tired and wanted to give up. (9)_______, I remembered why we were there. The park is home to many birds, and they need a clean place to live.

After three hours, the park looked completely different. Some visitors even (10)_______ us and said we were doing a great job. This experience taught me that small actions can make a big (11)_______. I have realized that everyone (12)_______ play a part in protecting our planet.

questions.explanation: 根据后文'said we were doing a great job'（说我们做得棒），可知游客是在'表扬'我们。praise表扬，stop阻止，join加入，refuse拒绝。语境线索：'doing a great job'对应积极评价。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last term, our school set up an Environmental Protection Club. As a member, I have learned a lot about how to protect our environment.

One Saturday, we organized a clean-up activity in the city park. When we arrived, we were shocked to see so much rubbish everywhere. Plastic bottles and paper bags were scattered under the trees.

We quickly got to work. Some students used tools to pick up the rubbish, (8)_______ others sorted it into different bags. At first, I felt tired and wanted to give up. (9)_______, I remembered why we were there. The park is home to many birds, and they need a clean place to live.

After three hours, the park looked completely different. Some visitors even (10)_______ us and said we were doing a great job. This experience taught me that small actions can make a big (11)_______. I have realized that everyone (12)_______ play a part in protecting our planet.

questions.explanation: 固定搭配make a difference意为'有影响、起作用'，符合句意'小行动可以产生大影响'。make a mistake犯错，make a decision做决定，make progress取得进步（progress不可数，前面不能加a）。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last term, our school set up an Environmental Protection Club. As a member, I have learned a lot about how to protect our environment.

One Saturday, we organized a clean-up activity in the city park. When we arrived, we were shocked to see so much rubbish everywhere. Plastic bottles and paper bags were scattered under the trees.

We quickly got to work. Some students used tools to pick up the rubbish, (8)_______ others sorted it into different bags. At first, I felt tired and wanted to give up. (9)_______, I remembered why we were there. The park is home to many birds, and they need a clean place to live.

After three hours, the park looked completely different. Some visitors even (10)_______ us and said we were doing a great job. This experience taught me that small actions can make a big (11)_______. I have realized that everyone (12)_______ play a part in protecting our planet.

questions.explanation: 句意：我意识到每个人都应该在保护地球中发挥作用。should表示'应该'，强调责任和义务，符合语境。must必须（语气太强），can能够，need需要（通常作实义动词need to do）。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:

We quickly got to work. Some students used tools to pick up the rubbish, (8)_______ others sorted it into different bags. At first, I felt tired and wanted to give up. (9)_______, I remembered why we were there. The park is home to many birds, and they need a clean place to live.

After three hours, the park looked completely different. Some visitors even (10)_______ us and said we were doing a great job. This experience taught me that small actions can make a big (11)_______. I have realized that everyone (12)_______ play a part in protecting our planet.

questions.stem: How many hours do teenagers spend on screens daily according to the study?
questions.explanation: 细节理解题。根据文章第二段'teenagers spend an average of 7.5 hours on screens every day'可知，青少年平均每天花7.5小时在屏幕上。解题技巧：细节题要准确定位关键词'daily/hours'，在原文中找到完全对应或同义替换的句子，注意数字信息。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
In recent years, smartphones have become an important part of our daily life. However, a new study shows that excessive (过度的) screen time may have negative effects on teenagers' health.

According to the research, teenagers spend an average of 7.5 hours on screens every day. This includes time spent on phones, computers, and TVs. Dr. Li, the lead researcher, explains that looking at screens for too long can cause eye problems and poor sleep quality.

Interestingly, the study also found that teenagers who spend more than 3 hours on social media are more likely to feel anxious. 'They constantly compare themselves with others online,' says Dr. Li. 'This can lead to low self-esteem (自尊).'

However, the researchers don't suggest giving up technology completely. Instead, they recommend setting time limits. For example, teenagers should avoid using phones one hour before bedtime. Parents are also advised to encourage outdoor activities to balance screen time.

The key is finding a healthy balance between the digital world and real life. As one participant said, 'I feel happier when I spend time with friends face-to-face rather than just chatting online.'

questions.stem: The underlined word 'excessive' in Paragraph 1 probably means ______.
questions.explanation: 词义猜测题。根据上下文，'excessive screen time'后提到'may have negative effects'（可能有负面影响），且第二段解释'looking at screens for too long'（看屏幕太久），可知excessive意为'过多的、过度的'。limited有限的，necessary必要的，proper适当的。解题技巧：通过因果线索（negative effects）和同义解释（too long）推断词义。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:
In recent years, smartphones have become an important part of our daily life. However, a new study shows that excessive (过度的) screen time may have negative effects on teenagers' health.

According to the research, teenagers spend an average of 7.5 hours on screens every day. This includes time spent on phones, computers, and TVs. Dr. Li, the lead researcher, explains that looking at screens for too long can cause eye problems and poor sleep quality.

Interestingly, the study also found that teenagers who spend more than 3 hours on social media are more likely to feel anxious. 'They constantly compare themselves with others online,' says Dr. Li. 'This can lead to low self-esteem (自尊).'

However, the researchers don't suggest giving up technology completely. Instead, they recommend setting time limits. For example, teenagers should avoid using phones one hour before bedtime. Parents are also advised to encourage outdoor activities to balance screen time.

The key is finding a healthy balance between the digital world and real life. As one participant said, 'I feel happier when I spend time with friends face-to-face rather than just chatting online.'

questions.stem: What can we infer from the passage?
options: A. All teenagers should stop using smartphones immediately.
B. Social media is completely bad for teenagers.
C. Face-to-face communication may be better for mental health.
D. Parents should forbid their children from using technology.
questions.explanation: 推理判断题。根据最后一段'I feel happier when I spend time with friends face-to-face rather than just chatting online'可推断，面对面交流可能对心理健康更有益。A、B、D都过于绝对，与原文'don't suggest giving up technology completely'（不建议完全放弃科技）不符。解题技巧：推理题要基于原文但不过度推断，排除绝对化选项（all/completely/forbid）。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:
In recent years, smartphones have become an important part of our daily life. However, a new study shows that excessive (过度的) screen time may have negative effects on teenagers' health.

According to the research, teenagers spend an average of 7.5 hours on screens every day. This includes time spent on phones, computers, and TVs. Dr. Li, the lead researcher, explains that looking at screens for too long can cause eye problems and poor sleep quality.

Interestingly, the study also found that teenagers who spend more than 3 hours on social media are more likely to feel anxious. 'They constantly compare themselves with others online,' says Dr. Li. 'This can lead to low self-esteem (自尊).'

However, the researchers don't suggest giving up technology completely. Instead, they recommend setting time limits. For example, teenagers should avoid using phones one hour before bedtime. Parents are also advised to encourage outdoor activities to balance screen time.

The key is finding a healthy balance between the digital world and real life. As one participant said, 'I feel happier when I spend time with friends face-to-face rather than just chatting online.'

questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: B. How to Use Social Media Safely
C. Screen Time and Teenagers' Health
questions.explanation: 主旨大意题。文章主要讨论屏幕时间对青少年健康的影响（视力、睡眠、心理健康）及建议。A只涉及历史，B只涉及社交媒体安全，D只讲优点，都过于片面。C选项'屏幕时间与青少年健康'全面概括了主题。解题技巧：标题要覆盖全文主要内容，避免以偏概全；通常出现频率高的关键词（screen time/health/teenagers）会出现在正确选项中。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
In recent years, smartphones have become an important part of our daily life. However, a new study shows that excessive (过度的) screen time may have negative effects on teenagers' health.

According to the research, teenagers spend an average of 7.5 hours on screens every day. This includes time spent on phones, computers, and TVs. Dr. Li, the lead researcher, explains that looking at screens for too long can cause eye problems and poor sleep quality.

Interestingly, the study also found that teenagers who spend more than 3 hours on social media are more likely to feel anxious. 'They constantly compare themselves with others online,' says Dr. Li. 'This can lead to low self-esteem (自尊).'

However, the researchers don't suggest giving up technology completely. Instead, they recommend setting time limits. For example, teenagers should avoid using phones one hour before bedtime. Parents are also advised to encourage outdoor activities to balance screen time.

The key is finding a healthy balance between the digital world and real life. As one participant said, 'I feel happier when I spend time with friends face-to-face rather than just chatting online.'

options: A. First, maintain a regular sleep schedule.
B. Even 20 minutes of sport every day is helpful.
C. However, sleep is equally important.
D. Avoid eating too much junk food like chips or candy.
E. In addition, you should drink plenty of water.
questions.explanation: 下文提到'Getting up early...Try to wake up at the same time'（早起、同一时间起床），说明本段讲规律作息。A选项'First, maintain a regular sleep schedule'（首先，保持规律的睡眠时间表）作为段首句总领全段，且First与下文的Second、Third形成顺序衔接。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
It's important for students to keep healthy. Here are some useful tips.

(17)_______. Getting up early gives you enough time to prepare for the day. Try to wake up at the same time every morning, even on weekends.

Second, eat a balanced diet. (18)_______. Also, remember to eat more fruits and vegetables. They provide vitamins that your body needs.

Third, exercise regularly. You don't need to run for hours. (19)_______. Walking to school or riding a bike can also be good choices.

(20)_______. Don't stay up late studying or playing games. A good night's sleep helps your brain work better the next day.

options: A. First, maintain a regular sleep schedule.
B. Even 20 minutes of sport every day is helpful.
C. However, sleep is equally important.
D. Avoid eating too much junk food like chips or candy.
E. In addition, you should drink plenty of water.
questions.explanation: 本段主题是'eat a balanced diet'（均衡饮食）。D选项'Avoid eating too much junk food'（避免吃太多垃圾食品）与饮食相关，且与下文'Also, remember to eat more fruits'（也要多吃水果）形成并列补充关系，先讲不要吃什么，再讲要吃什么。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
It's important for students to keep healthy. Here are some useful tips.

(17)_______. Getting up early gives you enough time to prepare for the day. Try to wake up at the same time every morning, even on weekends.

Second, eat a balanced diet. (18)_______. Also, remember to eat more fruits and vegetables. They provide vitamins that your body needs.

Third, exercise regularly. You don't need to run for hours. (19)_______. Walking to school or riding a bike can also be good choices.

(20)_______. Don't stay up late studying or playing games. A good night's sleep helps your brain work better the next day.

options: A. First, maintain a regular sleep schedule.
B. Even 20 minutes of sport every day is helpful.
C. However, sleep is equally important.
D. Avoid eating too much junk food like chips or candy.
E. In addition, you should drink plenty of water.
questions.explanation: 本段主题是'exercise regularly'（规律锻炼）。前句说'You don't need to run for hours'（不需要跑几个小时），B选项'Even 20 minutes of sport every day is helpful'（即使每天20分钟运动也有帮助）承接上文，说明运动不需要太长时间，少量也有效。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
It's important for students to keep healthy. Here are some useful tips.

(17)_______. Getting up early gives you enough time to prepare for the day. Try to wake up at the same time every morning, even on weekends.

Second, eat a balanced diet. (18)_______. Also, remember to eat more fruits and vegetables. They provide vitamins that your body needs.

Third, exercise regularly. You don't need to run for hours. (19)_______. Walking to school or riding a bike can also be good choices.

(20)_______. Don't stay up late studying or playing games. A good night's sleep helps your brain work better the next day.

options: A. First, maintain a regular sleep schedule.
B. Even 20 minutes of sport every day is helpful.
C. However, sleep is equally important.
D. Avoid eating too much junk food like chips or candy.
E. In addition, you should drink plenty of water.
questions.explanation: 下文提到'Don't stay up late...A good night's sleep'（不要熬夜、良好的睡眠），说明本段转折讲睡眠的重要性。C选项'However, sleep is equally important'（然而，睡眠同样重要）用However转折，引出睡眠话题，与前面的饮食、运动形成并列但转折强调的关系。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
It's important for students to keep healthy. Here are some useful tips.

(17)_______. Getting up early gives you enough time to prepare for the day. Try to wake up at the same time every morning, even on weekends.

Second, eat a balanced diet. (18)_______. Also, remember to eat more fruits and vegetables. They provide vitamins that your body needs.

Third, exercise regularly. You don't need to run for hours. (19)_______. Walking to school or riding a bike can also be good choices.

(20)_______. Don't stay up late studying or playing games. A good night's sleep helps your brain work better the next day.
